public_reputation: Known locally through day-to-day work as inventory steward.
manner: Views problems as maintenance loads, throughput failures, or structural weaknesses before anything else. In Transpolity, that reads as someone boundary-crossing and adaptive, speaking to whichever audience or protocol is presently in front of them, and the habits of a inventory steward still shape how they handle timing, silence, and attention.
daily_function: Performs the ongoing duties of inventory steward in and around Twilight Threadmill, where the role has practical consequences even when nothing dramatic is happening.
current_agenda: Keep Twilight Threadmill workable through the obligations of inventory steward while managing the immediate pressure of institutional scrutiny in Transpolity.
hidden_pressure: Must keep throughput credible under conditions that reward deferral more than repair. Around Twilight Threadmill, that usually means carrying institutional scrutiny, reputation risk, daily strain without letting the role of inventory steward visibly slip.

adventure_hooks.summary: Kest Mere needs outside help before wear, sabotage, or bottleneck pressure turns into a public failure in Twilight Threadmill.
Working with Kest Mere can draw the party into crew discipline, resource shortage, or blame transfer around Twilight Threadmill.

support_scope_notes: Promoted from a preexisting named interface into the unified full-content NPC architecture. Sparse unsupported fields remain intentionally restrained.
public_signal: Tool wear, work scars, or system-focused habits make Kest Mere visibly responsible for keeping something larger from failing.

encounter_context.usual_environment: Twilight Threadmill: worksites, depots, spans, loading points, maintenance corridors, or workshops
